_meta.parentTaskTitle: Learn the seven conditions of the Shahada (Ilm, Yaqin, Qabul, Inqiyad, Sidq, Ikhlas, Muhabbah)
_meta.claudeScholarQuery: For a MILOS subtask titled 'Inqiyad (Submission) — act upon it' under the parent task 'Learn the seven conditions of the Shahada (Ilm, Yaqin, Qabul, Inqiyad, Sidq, Ikhlas, Muhabbah)' (module: faith_shahada_core), propose exactly 2 NotebookLM queries to a Quran/Hadith primary-source corpus: (1) Quran-biased — name likely primary ayat that directly or contextually anchor this subtask; (2) Hadith-biased — name likely Sahih hadith (Bukhari/Muslim preferred) that directly or contextually anchor this subtask. Return the queries inside a <queries></queries> block as a numbered list, each one sentence, ready to paste into a retrieval system. Do NOT answer the queries — query formulation only.

muslimScholarQueries.query: Identify primary Quranic verses, specifically An-Nisa 4:65 and Luqman 31:22, that define the condition of Inqiyad as the total submission and practical compliance required for valid faith.

Retrieve Sahih hadith from Bukhari and Muslim, including the narration regarding the submission of one's desires to the prophetic message, to anchor the necessity of acting upon the testimony of faith.

sources.arabic: ۞ وَمَن يُسْلِمْ وَجْهَهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقَىٰ
sources.translation: And whoever submits his face [i.e., self] to God while he is a doer of good - then he has grasped the most trustworthy handhold. And to God will be the outcome of [all] matters.
sources.rationale: The verse names Inqiyad in its classical Quranic form: yuslim wajhahu — submitting the self — paired with muhsin (doing good), binding submission to action. Directly scripts the subtask's 'act upon it' by equating acting-upon-submission with 'grasping the most trustworthy handhold.' Bayyinah + direct.

notebookTrace.query: Luqman 31:22 defines Inqiyad as total submission and practical compliance required for valid faith

sources.arabic: إِنَّمَا كَانَ قَوْلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذَا دُعُوا إِلَى اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن يَقُولُوا سَمِعْنَا وَأَطَعْنَا
sources.translation: The only statement of the [true] believers when they are called to God and His Messenger to judge between them is that they say, 'We hear and we obey.' And those are the successful.
sources.rationale: 'Sami'na wa ata'na' is the verbal signature of Inqiyad — hearing and obeying as one motion. Directly supplies the phrase MILOS users should recognise as the active form of the Shahada's submission-condition. Bayyinah + direct.

notebookTrace.query: Luqman 31:22 defines Inqiyad as total submission and practical compliance required for valid faith

sources.translation: Messenger of Allah, tell me about Islam a thing which might dispense with the necessity of my asking anybody after you. He replied: Say I affirm my faith in Allah and then remain steadfast to it.
sources.rationale: The hadith of Sufyan bin Abdullah al-Thaqafi compresses Inqiyad into two motions — declare, then remain steadfast (istiqamah). Directly names acting-upon-the-testimony as the follow-through of the Shahada itself. Bayyinah (Sahih Muslim) + direct.

notebookTrace.query: Sahih hadith regarding the submission of one's desires to the prophetic message, necessity of acting upon the testimony

_groundedBarCheck.reason: 3 entries; all Bayyinah; all direct. Bar met. The Mu'adh/Bedouin/Sunnah-adherence narrations appeared in MS but with descriptive rather than fully verbatim matn paired to canonical refs — only Muslim 0062 carried both a canonical ref and a directly quoted matn suitable for binding.
